Portfolio Analytics

Your investments.
Properly analysed.

A single-file, no-install portfolio dashboard for multi-exchange investors. Import your transactions, get live prices, and see the full picture — returns, dividends, risk, and drawdown — in one place.

Try the live demo View on GitHub
SGX · HKEX · LSE
+ NYSE · NASDAQ · KLSE
Single file
no install · no backend
📊
Live prices
EOD closes fetched from Yahoo Finance each session via a proxy chain with local cache fallback.
🔒
Fully private
Your transactions never leave your browser. No server, no account, no analytics.
💱
Multi-currency
SGD, USD, HKD, GBP, MYR — live FX rates, all converted to your reference currency.
📁
No install
One HTML file. Open it, import a CSV, done. No npm, no Python, no dependencies.
🛡️
Your data never leaves your browser. Transaction history is held in memory and localStorage on your own machine. The only outbound calls are to Yahoo Finance for prices and dividends.sg for upcoming ex-dates — nothing about your holdings is ever transmitted elsewhere.
Features
Everything in one view
Ten tabs, all interconnected. Data flows from your transactions through to every chart, metric, and table automatically.
Dashboard
Your portfolio at a glance
The dashboard pulls together NAV, total return, unrealised P&L, dividends, and day change into a single view refreshed every session.
  • 10 hero KPIs — NAV, XIRR, open return, closed return, overall return, day change, capital gain, dividend YTD, projected year-end, total dividends
  • Daily change chart — weekly, monthly, and 3-month bar chart with each position's contribution
  • Last session movers — top gainers and losers vs. prior EOD close
  • Upcoming dividends — next 60 days, declared ex-dates from dividends.sg
Holdings
Every position, fully accounted
A currency-grouped summary at the top, then a detailed row per position — EOD close sourced live from Yahoo Finance, cost basis from your transactions.
  • Portfolio summary by currency — current value, day change, P&L, dividends received, and yield on cost per currency
  • Per-position detail — shares, avg cost, EOD close, day change (price + SGD), value, P&L, and return %
  • Click any row to open a full stock card with price history, FIFO lot breakdown, and transaction history
  • Filter by asset type · sort by weight, P&L, or return
Performance
Returns that mean something
TWR strips out the timing of your deposits so you can see pure investment performance. XIRR weights by when capital was actually deployed. Both are benchmarked against ES3 (Nikko STI ETF) on a like-for-like basis.
  • TWR vs XIRR — the gap tells you whether your timing added or cost value
  • ES3 benchmark — switch-fund XIRR, TWR diff, beta, Jensen's alpha, tracking error, information ratio
  • Risk ratios — Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, win rate (daily)
  • Monthly heatmap — colour-coded return grid, scrollable back to inception
  • Range controls: YTD · 1Y · 3Y · 5Y · 10Y · All
Dividends
Income, tracked in full
Dividend history sourced from Yahoo Finance for all exchanges, with upcoming ex-dates from dividends.sg for SGX holdings. Manual entry for anything Yahoo misses.
  • All-time total, YTD, projected to year-end — and a monthly average
  • Yield on cost — forward-looking, per position and aggregate
  • Annual income CAGR — how fast your income stream is growing
  • Stacked bar chart — income by year, broken down by position
  • Annual table — income in SGD + local currency, vs prior year, yield on cost, yield on NAV
  • DRIP support — reinvested dividends create new lots automatically
Exposure & Analysis
Where your money actually is
A full breakdown of exposure by exchange, asset type, and currency — with a live portfolio summary table showing current value, P&L, dividends, total return, and XIRR per exchange.
  • Portfolio summary by exchange — expandable rows showing each position
  • Three donut charts — by asset type, exchange, and currency
  • Currency detail — live FX rates, SGD equivalent value, and portfolio weight per currency
  • Concentration chart — top 10 positions by weight
Stock Card
Drill into any position
Click any holding to open a detailed card — full price history with your buy and sell markers overlaid, current lot breakdown, and a complete transaction log with running balance.
  • Price history chart — EOD closes back to your first buy, with ▲ buy and ▼ sell markers
  • Current position — FIFO lots with cost/share, current price, cost basis (SGD), market value, unrealised P&L, return %, and hold duration
  • Transaction history — every buy and sell with value (local + SGD), capital gain, fees, and running share balance
  • EOD close · day change · realised P&L · unrealised P&L · dividends · total return
Realised P&L
What you've actually made
A complete accounting of every closed position — realised capital gain, dividends received on shares since sold, and total return including both. WAC cost basis to match Singapore broker displays.
  • Summary by currency — cost, proceeds, capital gain, gain %, dividends, total return
  • Sell history — one row per sell, FIFO lots, expandable lot detail
  • Open positions — unrealised gain + dividends received = total return for each current holding
  • WAC cost basis · partial sells supported · FIFO lot expansion on click
Transactions
Your full transaction log
Every buy and sell in one searchable, sortable table — with a net cash flow chart showing capital deployed and returned over time.
  • Net cash flow chart — buys and sells by date, 3M · 6M · YTD range
  • Transaction history — date, action, symbol, exchange, shares, price, fees, value, currency, notes
  • Search by symbol or name · filter by action · sort newest or oldest first
  • Add, edit, or delete any transaction directly in the app
Corp Actions & Settings
Handle splits, mergers & more
Manual entry for corporate actions that Yahoo Finance doesn't retroactively adjust — splits, bonus issues, rights, and MCB redemptions. Applied automatically on every load.
  • Corp actions — splits, bonus shares, rights issues with ratio entry
  • Holdings classification — set asset type and sector for each position, persists across sessions
  • Price overrides — manually set current price for unlisted instruments (e.g. bonds, private placements)
  • Multiple portfolio support — run several portfolios side by side or as a combined view

Get started
Up and running in minutes
Option 1 — No install
Use the live app
Open the GitHub Pages link in any browser. Click Load demo to explore with sample data, or Import CSV to load your own transactions.
Option 2 — Run locally
Clone and serve
Clone the repo and start a local server. Any static server works — Python's built-in one is the simplest:
git clone https://github.com/GumbyOne/ Gumby-s-Portfolio-analytics.git python3 -m http.server 8080
Option 3 — Your own data
Import a CSV
Download the blank template from the Import tab, fill in your transactions in Excel or Google Sheets, save as CSV, and import. Only symbol and date columns are required.
